text:
Market today: Mild risk-on tone but no clear catalyst. The real driver is AI infrastructure narrative staying warm (Nvidia/OpenAI headlines) while macro stays murky - oil weakness and Iran progress reduce inflation fear, which is a soft positive for rate-sensitive tech. Nothing explosive either way.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest problem - 3x leveraged, decay is eating you alive, and you're on margin so a bad day hits triple-hard; stop at $200 is your line. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO, already down -13.72% with a stop at $21.38 - you're basically at the wire right now. SMH (-2.5%) is a quiet bleed worth watching; chips broadly soft.

Watch: 1) SOXL $200 stop - if semis wobble it gets there fast on margin. 2) Any Nvidia/chip-specific news around AI compute deals; that's what flips SMH and SOXL quickly.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL) are the only real job today - protect those or cut them before margin does it for you.
plays: SOXL: Honor the $200 stop, no exceptions on margin. Down 14.5% on a 3x product means underlying semis are already hurting you hard.
SPAL: Stop is $21.38 and price is $21.70 - you have almost no cushion. Decide now if you want to tighten or exit; don't let a leveraged IPO bleed you on margin.
No new buys today - PANW scanner setup (entry 351.34-351.69, stop 350.62) is cleanest grade-A pattern but no fresh catalyst specific to it today; watch, don't chase.
